scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within bioinformatics data processing scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: NCBI BLAST API usage is standard and methodologically correct for pairwise sequence alignment.

code_usability.detail: scripts/main.py confirmed fixed: all XML element .text accesses now use None guards (e.g., int(hit_len.text or '0'), float(bit_score.text or '0.0')) throughout parse_blast_xml. The v3 P0 TypeError risk is fully resolved.

functional_suitability.note: Core bioinformatics data processing functionality is complete; minor gaps in edge-case handling or output completeness exist.

reliability.note: Input Validation at Step 1 of Workflow; scope check fires before any processing; None guards now applied in script — malformed BLAST XML handled gracefully.

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for bioinformatics data processing tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Workflow Step 1 explicitly gates on scope before any processing; MSA and phylogenetics alternatives documented.

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical bioinformatics data processing use cases.

security.note: No credential exposure or injection vectors identified; input handling stays within bioinformatics data processing boundaries.

maintainability.note: None guard pattern now applied in script — parse_blast_xml is robust to malformed XML; SKILL.md Input Validation note matches implementation.

agent_specific.note: MSA tool redirect (MUSCLE, CLUSTALW, MAFFT) and phylogenetics redirect (IQ-TREE) in out-of-scope response.

recommendations.title: No NCBI API rate limiting or API key guidance documented
recommendations.problem: NCBI BLAST API has usage limits (3 requests/second without API key, 10/second with key). The skill does not document these limits or recommend using an API key for production use.
recommendations.root_cause: API etiquette was not addressed in the initial design.
recommendations.fix: Add to Security Notes or Error Handling: 'NCBI BLAST API rate limit: 3 requests/second without API key. For production use, register for an NCBI API key at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/ and pass it via --api-key.'

recommendations.title: No input sequence character validation documented
recommendations.problem: Security Notes mentions input sequences are validated against allowed character patterns, but no validation logic or allowed character set is documented in SKILL.md.
recommendations.root_cause: Security note references validation without specifying the allowed pattern.
recommendations.fix: Add to Input Validation or Security Notes: 'DNA sequences must match [ACGTNRYSWKMBDHV-]+; protein sequences must match [ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWY*-]+. Sequences failing validation are rejected before submission.'
